readables.title: A Torn Journal Page
readables.body:
[i]The ink has run in the damp, and half the words are gone.[/i]

They buried the last of the Liziti dead at dusk, but the ground would not lie still. By the third night I heard scratching beneath the Perrow stones, soft as a cat at a door.

If you are reading this — and if the fog has not yet taken your wits — do not dig.

readables.title: Chronicle of Perrow
pages:
[b]Chronicle of Perrow[/b]
[i]Volume the First[/i]

In the years before the fog, Perrow was a town of bells and bargains — small, and well content to be small. Its graves were tended, its saints were sung, and no traveller thought it strange.

[b]The Long Autumn[/b]

Then came the season that would not turn. The wells went brackish; the bells cracked one by one; and the saints, when called upon, did not answer. The living grew few, and the buried grew restless.

[b]What Remains[/b]

What remains of Perrow is written in its graves. Read them, traveller, and count yourself among the warned — for a town can fall apart as surely as any man, and just as quietly.
